primary_sources.relevance: threshold trust, revocable keys, key rotation, signed metadata and unique key identifiers
authenticated checkpoints and same-size divergent-root equivocation semantics

finding: A witness quorum is not lifecycle-safe unless each witness record is independently signed by a named key epoch and evaluated against explicit validity and revocation intervals. Conflicting authenticated records should be exported as a deterministic, digest-bound packet rather than collapsed into a boolean failure.
design_implication: Authenticate the witness observation separately from the underlying checkpoint signature; bind witness_id, key_id, observation time, and checkpoint bytes into the signature; fail closed outside the key epoch or after revocation; preserve same-size divergent-root records in a portable packet with an explicit trust limit and falsification route.

cases: signature accepted inside configured key epoch
record rejected when relabeled to another key id
portable packet emitted only for authenticated same-size divergent roots

limits: No production witness keys were used.
Local tests do not establish independent witness operation or global log consistency.
The packet authenticates configured signatures and conflict structure; it does not identify cause or prove the underlying checkpoint is true.
Cryptographic verification still depends on securely distributing and updating the witness key policy.
falsification_route: Reject the design if an independent implementation can accept a record outside its key interval, after revocation, under a relabeled key id, with an invalid signature, or can emit a packet without two authenticated records at the same tree size with divergent roots.
privacy_boundary: Synthetic keys and synthetic checkpoint hashes only; no private chat, health, credential, deployment secret, or identity-bearing material is included.
